title: Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain
summary: A rain-watch plan for homes below burned slopes where short intense rainfall can mobilize mud, ash, rocks, and tree debris with little warning.
content:
A rain-watch plan for homes below burned slopes where short intense rainfall can mobilize mud, ash, rocks, and tree debris with little warning.

Use the materials as a compact field kit. Work through the steps in order, pause whenever a warning or stop condition applies, and write down the outcome so another person can continue safely.
steps: Identify burned slopes, gullies, bridges, culverts, and roads between the home and safer high ground before rain begins.
Set an early departure trigger based on official warnings, intense rainfall, rising muddy water, blocked drains, or unusual roaring sounds.
Move people, animals, medicines, and mobility equipment before channels begin flowing; use the preselected route that avoids gullies and low crossings.
After the rain, remain outside channels and wait for official road and slope assessment before returning.
warnings: Do not wait to see mud before evacuating a known burn-scar channel.
Never shelter in a basement or ground-floor room facing the slope.
common_mistakes: Starting Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain without first checking immediate danger and official instructions.
Failing to record time, location, responsible person, and unresolved risks for Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain.
alternatives: Use a paper-only version of Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain when power or connectivity is unavailable.
Assign one trained helper to simplify Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain when the group is stressed or short-handed.
failure_signs: Conditions become more dangerous while carrying out Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain.
Critical information cannot be confirmed or the assigned helper cannot continue Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain safely.
when_not_to_use: Do not continue Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain when it requires entering an unstable, contaminated, flooded, burning, electrically hazardous, or officially closed area.
Do not improvise medical, electrical, structural, or rescue actions beyond your training while using Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain.
short_term: ['Within the first operational period, Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain should produce a dated status, one responsible person, and a safe next action.', 'Repeat the essential check for Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain whenever conditions, official advice, or available resources change.']
long_term: ['Include lessons from Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain in the household or community emergency plan and resupply list.', 'Review the records from Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain after the event and correct gaps before the next emergency.']
if_method_fails: ['If Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain cannot be completed, move to the safest verified location, notify the responsible contact, and record what remains unresolved.', 'Use official emergency services or qualified specialists when the limits of Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain are reached.']
environment_notes: ['Adapt Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain to local terrain, weather, building type, transport access, language, disability needs, and official restrictions.', 'In Greece, use 112 for urgent emergencies and follow Civil Protection or local-authority instructions relevant to Post-wildfire debris-flow watch during heavy rain.']
